source:
- 3.2.2 Stack Protocol Reset (M) An Opal SSC compliant Storage Device SHALL support the Stack Protocol Reset command. Refer to [2] for details.
- 3.2.3 TPER_RESET command (M)
title: Stack Protocol Reset (M)

source: If the TPER_RESET command is enabled, it SHALL cause the following before the TPer accepts the next IF-SEND or IF-RECV command:
title: Stack Protocol Reset (M)

source: - a) all dynamically allocated ComIDs SHALL return to the Inactive state;
title: Stack Protocol Reset (M)
Stack Protocol Reset (M)

source: The TPER_RESET command is delivered by the transport IF-SEND command. If the TPER_RESET command is enabled, the TPer SHALL accept and acknowledge it at the interface level. If the TPER_RESET command is disabled, the TPer SHALL abort it at the interface level with the “Other Invalid Command Parameter” status (see [4]). There is no IF-RECV response to the TPER_RESET command.
title: Stack Protocol Reset (M)

source: The TPER_RESET command is defined in Table 13. The Transfer Length SHALL be non-zero. All data transferred SHALL be ignored.
title: Stack Protocol Reset (M)

source: FIELD VALUE Command IF-SEND Protocol ID 0x02 Transfer Length Non-zero ComID 0x0004
title: Stack Protocol Reset (M)

source:
| FIELD | VALUE |
| --- | --- |
| Command | IF-SEND |
| Protocol ID | 0x02 |
| Transfer Length | Non-zero |
| ComID | 0x0004 |
title: Stack Protocol Reset (M)
